title: Catcher's Sky-High Catch Breaks Records
story: Baseballs usually soar from the pitcher's mound, but in 1930, one fell from the sky! On April 1, Chicago Cubs catcher Leo Hartnett made an incredible catch that left fans amazed. title: The Universe's Explosive Origin Story
story: Boom! In 1948, three brilliant scientists shook up our understanding of the cosmos. Ralph Alpher, Hans Bethe, and George Gamow proposed the 'Big Bang' theory in a scientific journal called Physical Review. Their groundbreaking idea suggested that the entire universe began with an enormous explosion, expanding from a tiny point into the vast space we see today. This cosmic revelation happened on April 1, but it was no joke!

story: Hockey fans cheered as Mike Bossy of the New York Islanders made history in 1978. The talented right wing became the first NHL rookie to score 50 goals in a season. title: Gretzky Skates Past Orr's Record
story: Hockey legend Wayne Gretzky glided into the record books on April 1, 1981. The Edmonton Oilers star notched his 103rd assist of the season during a 4-4 tie with the Colorado Rockies. This incredible feat shattered Bobby Orr's decade-old record for most assists in a single NHL season. Gretzky's playmaking skills were on full display, cementing his status as one of hockey's greatest passers.

Most goals in a single NHL season

Most hat tricks in a single NHL season
activity_answer: Most assists in a single NHL season

title: Halley's Comet Zooms Past Earth

title: Eyes in the Sky: TIROS 1 Takes Flight
story: Zoom! A rocket blasted off, carrying a special passenger: TIROS 1. This wasn't just any satellite. On April 1, 1960, it became our planet's first weather watcher in space. It was a giant leap for forecasting, helping us better prepare for rain, snow, and sunshine.

title: The Birth of Modern Manga
story: Pow! Zap! A new era of Japanese comics exploded onto the scene on April 1, 1947. Osamu Tezuka, a young artist with big dreams, released 'Shin Takarajima' (New Treasure Island). This groundbreaking book wasn't just any comic - it was the first manga published in tankōbon form, a thick, standalone volume. Tezuka's dynamic art style and cinematic storytelling captivated readers, setting the stage for manga's global popularity today.
activity_question: What was special about 'Shin Takarajima' when it was released?
activity_choices: It was the first manga in tankōbon form
It was drawn by a robot
It was printed on gold paper
activity_answer: It was the first manga in tankōbon form
follow_up_question: Do you know what 'manga' means in English?

title: The Incredible 100-Year-Old Marathon Runner
story: Fauja Singh laced up his running shoes and hit the pavement, but he wasn't your average jogger. Born on April 1, 1911, in British India, Singh became known as the world's oldest marathon runner. title: Buried City Unearthed After Centuries
story: Shovels struck something unexpected in 1748. Spanish engineer Roque Joaquín de Alcubierre had stumbled upon an ancient wonder: the lost city of Pompeii! Buried by volcanic ash nearly 1700 years earlier, this Roman town lay hidden until April 1. Alcubierre's discovery launched years of careful digging, revealing remarkably preserved buildings, artwork, and a snapshot of daily life frozen in time.
activity_question: What did Roque Joaquín de Alcubierre discover on April 1, 1748?
bonus_fact: Pompeii was buried when nearby Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD.

title: Ancient Maya Victory Carved in Stone
story: Swords clash and shields clatter! In 792, the Maya city of Piedras Negras celebrated a big win. Their army had beaten their neighbors from Pomona in battle. To remember this victory forever, they carved the story into a tall stone called Stela 12. This special stone stood in Piedras Negras, telling everyone about their triumph on April 1.
activity_question: What did the Maya use to record their victory?
follow_up_question: Why do you think ancient civilizations carved important events into stone?
